payload.principle: Functional variants receive a utility bag as 2nd arg
payload.explanation: Second argument: `{ theme, tokens, props, font, fontFamily, fonts, context }`. `theme` is a proxy with `$`-prefixed keys; `tokens` has all categories; `props` are the component's current props; `font` is the resolved font object (may be undefined); `context` is the current styledContext for compound components.

payload.principle: Form + Form.Trigger enables accessible universal forms
payload.explanation: `<Form onSubmit={fn}>…<Form.Trigger asChild><Button /></Form.Trigger></Form>`. `Form.Trigger` calls `onSubmit` when pressed — works on native and web. Produces accessible markup on web (`<form>`). Use `asChild` to wrap any pressable element as the submit trigger.

payload.principle: createTokens defines the static design-system primitives
payload.explanation: `createTokens({ size: { sm: 10 }, color: { black: '#000' }, ... })` returns tokens you pass to createTamagui. Access anywhere with `getTokens().size.small` (returns a Variable with `.val` and `.variable`). Use `getTokens({ prefixed: true })['$size'].small` to get prefixed keys. `getVariable` returns CSS var on web, raw value on native.

payload.principle: Switch: toggle with native fallback and three implementation levels
payload.explanation: Props: `checked`/`defaultChecked`, `onCheckedChange`, `size`, `activeTheme`, `activeStyle`. `native` prop renders a platform-native switch on mobile. Three levels: Styled (default), Unstyled (`createSwitch`), Headless (`useSwitch` — zero core dep, returns `checkboxProps`, `checkboxRef`, hidden input for forms).

payload.principle: tamagui.build.ts is the single source of truth for bundlers
payload.explanation: Central config file read by all bundler plugins (metro, babel, vite, webpack) and the CLI — specifies config path, components to optimize, optional constants whitelist, and extraction options. Avoids duplicating the setup across multiple plugin configs.

payload.principle: Specific tokens let you add custom categories with dotted access
payload.explanation: Since v1.34 you can add arbitrary token categories like `icon: { small: 16, medium: 24 }`. Access with the category-first syntax: `<Stack width="$icon.small" />`. Use the `px()` helper from @tamagui/core for custom categories to emit `100px` on web and `100` on native; built-in size/space/radius already add px where needed.

payload.principle: Design system package: own npm module + tamagui-build + components array
payload.explanation: Design systems need their own package (can be private) so the compiler can import and analyze them. Use `@tamagui/build` (wraps esbuild+tsc) to build with JSX preserved. Set `module:jsx` and `sideEffects: ['*.css']` in package.json. Then add the package name to `components: ['@yourapp/ui', 'tamagui']` in bundler config.

payload.principle: Button: size prop cascades to text, icon, borderRadius, padding
payload.explanation: Tamagui Button accepts `size` (SizeTokens) — one prop sets padding, border radius, font size, icon size simultaneously via size-spread variants. Variants: `outlined` (transparent bg, visible border). Props: `icon`, `iconAfter`, `iconSize`, `scaleIcon`, `circular`, `chromeless`, `unstyled`. Defaults to `type="button"` to prevent form-submit. In v2, text styling uses `<Button.Text>` subcomponent.

payload.principle: Platform-specific file splits work with CLI and bundlers
payload.explanation: Create `animations.ts` (web) and `animations.native.ts` (native). `@tamagui/cli` automatically handles `.web.tsx`, `.native.tsx`, `.ios.tsx`, `.android.tsx` when pre-compiling. Metro/Expo handle these natively. Result: optimal code per platform without `Platform.select` in your source.

payload.principle: Tooltip: hover tooltip, web-only rendering
payload.explanation: Doesn't render on native (provides accessibility output instead). `restMs` = time cursor must rest before showing; `delay` = max time. `groupId` — tooltips in same group skip delay after first shows. `TooltipGroup` manages multi-tooltip delay behavior. `placement` has 12 options. Uses `PortalProvider` (modal=true by default).

payload.principle: Tokens are static variables; themes are dynamic scoped variables
payload.explanation: Tokens = base static values that never change (like global CSS vars). Themes = object maps whose values change contextually in the React tree (like scoped CSS vars). Tokens are fallbacks — when a theme lookup misses, Tamagui checks the matching token. Default categories: size, space, color, radius, zIndex, font, fontSize, lineHeight, letterSpace.

payload.principle: Design system packages need to be listed in components array
payload.explanation: The compiler knows how to optimize `styled()` components only from packages declared in `components: ['tamagui', '@yourapp/ui']`. If your design system is in its own package, add it there. Use `@tamagui/build` to build design-system packages with JSX preserved (`module:jsx`). Also add `sideEffects: ['*.css']` to prevent Webpack from stripping generated CSS.

payload.principle: Dialog.Scoping: mount at root, trigger from anywhere
payload.explanation: Add matching `scope="myDialog"` to both `<Dialog>` (placed at app root) and `<Dialog.Trigger>`. Lets you avoid React context issues in portals and improves performance for deeply nested triggers. Same pattern works for Popover and Tooltip.

payload.principle: forceStyle prop activates a pseudo state programmatically
payload.explanation: `<View forceStyle="hover">` forces hover styles even without hovering — useful for demos, visual regression tests, Storybook stories, or keyboard-driven navigation where you want the hover appearance without mouse presence.

payload.principle: Media queries live in createTamagui({ media }), no prefix at use
payload.explanation: Define `createTamagui({ media: { sm: { maxWidth: 800 } } })`. Use as `<Text $sm={{ color: 'blue' }} />`. V5 renames 2xl→xxl; max queries use kebab-case (`max-xxl`). Height queries like `height-sm`, `height-md`, `height-lg`, plus `touch` and `hoverable` for device capabilities.

payload.principle: accept: wrap non-Tamagui components (SVG, third-party) for token support
payload.explanation: `styled(SvgIcon, { accept: { fill: 'color' } })` — the `accept` option maps non-Tamagui prop names to Tamagui style categories, enabling token values like `fill="$blue10"`. Set `acceptsClassName: true` on web for CSS media query support and compile-time optimization for external components.

payload.principle: Prop order matters: later props override earlier ones
payload.explanation: `<View background="red" {...props} width={200} />` means background is default-overridable by props, but width is locked to 200. Same for variants: `<Square scale={3} huge />` applies huge last, so huge wins; `<Square huge scale={3} />` applies scale last. Making order meaningful is what lets variants expand to multiple properties without boilerplate.

payload.principle: XStack = flex row, YStack = flex column, ZStack = absolute overlap
payload.explanation: XStack = `flexDirection: 'row'`. YStack = `flexDirection: 'column'` (default). ZStack = absolute-positioned children stacked on top of each other. All extend View — accept all Tamagui style props. Use `gap="$4"` for spacing between children. `elevation` creates a platform-native-looking shadow that grows with size.

payload.principle: Groups: name a parent, style children based on its state or size
payload.explanation: Set `group` (unnamed) or `group="header"` (named) on a parent. Children target it via `$group-hover`, `$group-press`, `$group-focus` (pseudo), `$group-sm` (container query), or named variants like `$group-header-hover`. Container queries use the same breakpoints from `createTamagui({ media })`. Type named groups with `interface TypeOverride { groupNames(): 'card' | 'header' }`.

payload.principle: Sub-themes nest with parent_child naming
payload.explanation: Name themes `parentName_subName` and Tamagui lets them nest: `dark_green`, `light_green_subtle`. Wrap with `<Theme name="dark"><Theme name="green"><Button theme="subtle"/></Theme></Theme>`. Sub-themes can be subsets of parents — missing keys resolve upward. Also pass full names directly: `<Button theme="green_subtle"/>`.

payload.principle: Tree-shake themes JS in prod by relying on CSS hydration
payload.explanation: For production client bundles, export an empty themes object: `process.env.TAMAGUI_IS_SERVER ? themesIn : {} as typeof themesIn`. The Next.js plugin sets TAMAGUI_IS_SERVER automatically. Tamagui rehydrates by scanning CSS in the browser (low single-digit ms) and saves significant JS size. Only works when the getCSS output is served on initial load.

payload.principle: disableExtraction speeds up dev builds
payload.explanation: In `tamagui.build.ts` set `disableExtraction: true` for dev. This skips CSS extraction and relies on runtime — fast HMR. Enable it for production to get CSS output and tree-flattening. The `logTimings: true` option shows per-file compile times.

payload.principle: Design system pattern: styled() with variants + GetProps type export
payload.explanation: Create components with `styled(YStack, { variants: {…} })`, export `export type CircleProps = GetProps<typeof Circle>`. Re-export base components from `tamagui` or `@tamagui/core` for a single import surface. Test with `// debug` pragma to verify extraction: output should say `1 optimized, 1 flattened`.

payload.principle: Compiler is optional and never required to use Tamagui
payload.explanation: All Tamagui features work 100% at runtime. The compiler adds performance on top. Install it per bundler: `tamagui-loader` (webpack), `@tamagui/vite-plugin` (Vite), `@tamagui/babel-plugin` (Metro/Babel), `@tamagui/next-plugin` (Next.js legacy), or `@tamagui/cli` (any bundler via pre-compilation). Start without it; add later.

payload.principle: Native benchmarks: within 10% of vanilla React Native
payload.explanation: Tamagui without compiler averages 125ms (12% slower). With compiler, within ~5-10% of hand-optimized RN. The compiler is less impactful on native than web since native can't emit CSS, but it still removes hooks and object allocations from the render loop.

payload.principle: $-prefix selectors target parent state: media, theme, platform, group
payload.explanation: Use `$` on style keys to apply nested style objects conditionally: `$sm`/`$gtMd` (media queries), `$theme-dark` (top-level theme), `$platform-ios`/`$platform-native` (platform), `$group-*` (parent group state). Each accepts the full style-prop set. Works on native and web through CSS or runtime.

payload.principle: withStaticProperties attaches subcomponents to a parent
payload.explanation: `withStaticProperties(ButtonFrame, { Text: ButtonText, Icon: ButtonIcon, Props: Context.Provider })` returns one component you can use as `<Button.Text>` / `<Button.Icon>` / `<Button.Props>`. Standard compound component pattern that keeps import surface small and makes relationships visible in JSX.

payload.principle: Dialog: accessible modal with optional Sheet adaptation
payload.explanation: Anatomy: `Dialog > Dialog.Trigger > Dialog.Portal > Dialog.Overlay + Dialog.Content > Dialog.Title + Dialog.Description + Dialog.Close`. Title and Description required for accessibility. Dismiss: click outside, Escape, or Close. `modal={false}` removes focus trap and allows background interaction. Use `Dialog.Adapt when="max-md"` + `<Dialog.Sheet>` to render as a Sheet on mobile.

payload.principle: asChild passes all props to the single child element
payload.explanation: `asChild` on any Tamagui component renders without a wrapper — it passes all props (style, events, theme) to its direct child. `<Popover.Trigger asChild><MyButton /></Popover.Trigger>` makes MyButton open the Popover. Child must accept the passed props. Similar to Radix UI's asChild pattern.

payload.principle: SizableText and Paragraph add the size prop for theme-aware typography
payload.explanation: `SizableText` extends Text with a `size` spread variant that maps to font size, line height, letter spacing, and font weight — all from your createFont config. `Paragraph` extends SizableText with `tag='p'`, `color='$color'`, `userSelect='auto'`, `whiteSpace='normal'`. Use Paragraph for readable prose; SizableText for span-level inline text.

payload.principle: Component themes: name_ComponentName for targeted overrides
payload.explanation: Any `styled(View, { name: 'Circle' })` looks for a theme ending `_Circle`. Define `dark_Circle` or `dark_green_Circle` to change defaults only for Circles. Component-theme names must be capitalized. This lets library authors keep design authority while users customize per-component. Removes the `name` prop from defaultProps at runtime.

payload.principle: Typed variant keys: true/false, :string, :boolean, :number
payload.explanation: `true`/`false` create a boolean prop. `:string`, `:boolean`, `:number` are type hints — the variant function receives the typed value. `true`/`false` take precedence over `:boolean`. Example: `color: { ':string': (c) => ({ color: c, borderColor: c }) }` accepts any string color.

payload.principle: Exports: platform detection constants and helper hooks
payload.explanation: @tamagui/core exports: `isWeb`, `isBrowser`, `isServer`, `isClient`, `isChrome`, `isWebTouchable`, `isTouchable`, `useIsomorphicLayoutEffect`. Helpers: `getConfig`, `getTokens`, `getToken`, `getTokenValue`, `getVariable` (CSS var on web, raw on native), `isTamaguiComponent`, `isTamaguiElement`. Type helpers: `GetProps`, `GetRef`.

payload.principle: config.getCSS() extracts all generated CSS for SSR
payload.explanation: Call `config.getCSS()` server-side to get a string of all atomic CSS to inject into `<style>` tags or serve as a file. This includes token CSS variables, theme classes, and component styles. With React 19, non-static styles auto-inject via style tags at runtime. For production, generate and serve the CSS file for best performance.

payload.principle: Toast: universal feedback notification with native integrations
payload.explanation: `useToastController().show('Message', { duration, message })`. `ToastProvider` configures global duration (default 5000ms), swipe direction, native options. `ToastViewport` is a portal with positioning. Native: SPIndicator on iOS, ToastAndroid on Android, Notification API on web. Install `burnt` for native. Custom data via TS module augmentation.

payload.principle: Group: automatic border-radius management for button/input rows
payload.explanation: Wrap items in `Group.Item`. Group zeros out connecting-side radius between adjacent items. `size` on Group only sets borderRadius — child sizing is controlled by each child directly. `forcePlacement` on `Group.Item` overrides detection: 'first', 'center', 'last'. For dynamic children, use `useGroupItem` hook or map with index.

payload.principle: Adapt: render one component for web, another for native/mobile
payload.explanation: `<Adapt when="max-md"><Sheet>…<Adapt.Contents /></Sheet></Adapt>` — Tamagui renders the Adapt contents (Dialog/Popover/Select inner content) inside the wrapper when the condition is true. `Adapt.Contents` preserves all children and event handlers from the original. Condition can be any media query key.

payload.principle: createThemeBuilder for programmatic theme suites
payload.explanation: Three progressive helpers: (1) `createV5Theme` — quick defaults with optional palette overrides; (2) `createThemes` — palette-interpolation helpers with base/accent/children levels; (3) `createThemeBuilder` — low-level chainable API for full control over palettes, templates (map prop → palette index), and sub-theme hierarchy. Negative indices count backward. Build at compile time to shrink bundles.

payload.principle: Tamagui web benchmarks: ~3-6x faster than RNW, ~2x faster than Emotion
payload.explanation: Simple component render: Tamagui 0.018ms vs RNW 0.057ms vs Emotion 0.041ms. Updating variants: 0.02ms vs RNW 0.063ms vs SC 0.081ms. Inline styles (unique to Tamagui — only library that compiles them): 0.025ms vs RNW 0.06ms. Deep tree mounting: 18.61ms vs Emotion 42.49ms vs SC 51.4ms. Gains come largely from tree-flattening.

payload.principle: Dynamic themes: addTheme, updateTheme, replaceTheme
payload.explanation: `@tamagui/theme` exports `addTheme`, `updateTheme` (merges values), and `replaceTheme` (replaces whole theme). Dynamic changes apply on the client only — ignored during SSR. Useful for user-controlled palettes or lazy-loaded theme bundles.

payload.principle: useThemeName returns the current resolved theme name string
payload.explanation: `const name = useThemeName()` returns the current theme name (e.g. `'dark_green'`). Useful for conditional logic based on theme without subscribing to all theme values. Lighter alternative to `useTheme()` when you only need the name.

payload.principle: Next.js: use CLI build script for Turbopack, legacy plugin for Webpack
payload.explanation: Modern: `yarn add -D @tamagui/cli`, build script `tamagui build --target web ./src -- next build`. Configures transpilation for `@tamagui/lucide-icons-2`, sets Turbopack aliases for RN compatibility. Legacy Webpack: `withTamagui` plugin from `@tamagui/next-plugin`. Use `@tamagui/next-theme` with `useRootTheme` hook for SSR-safe light/dark.

payload.principle: Child variants can trigger parent variants via composition
payload.explanation: `styled(ParentStyled, { variants: { hero: { true: { large: true } } } })` — setting hero on the child activates the parent's `large` variant. Variant values can reference parent variants directly, composing styles without duplication.

payload.principle: Tamagui size scale is hybrid: gradual 1-10, exponential 11-16
payload.explanation: Most systems use pure exponential (2,4,8,16). Tamagui uses a gradual scale 1-10 so $2 is tiny UI, $4 is average, $5-$6 emphasized, $7+ for hero/CTA. Above 10 it turns exponential for chunky sizes. Three sub-1 quantum tokens ($0.25, $0.5, $0.75) exist for every group. All token groups (size, space, radius, font) align to the same 1-16 keys so components compose.

payload.principle: Catch-all variant: '...' handles any value not matched by others
payload.explanation: Mix typed cases with a fallback: `colorful: { true: { color: 'red' }, '...': (val: string) => ({ color: val }) }`. The catch-all runs only when the value doesn't match named keys. Useful for extending a preset set with arbitrary values.

payload.principle: Flat transform props: x, y, scale, rotate — no transform array
payload.explanation: Instead of `transform: [{translateX: 10}]` you write `x={10} y={20} scale={1.1} rotate="45deg"`. Supported: x, y, perspective, scale, scaleX, scaleY, skewX, skewY, matrix, rotate, rotateX, rotateY, rotateZ. These compile to the equivalent transform entries. Cleaner ergonomics and animation targets.

payload.principle: createFont: align keys across size, lineHeight, letterSpacing, weight
payload.explanation: Aligning the same key names across all font sub-categories enables SizableText `size` prop to look up matching values from all categories simultaneously. Example: size: { $4: 16 }, lineHeight: { $4: 24 } — `<SizableText size="$4" />` picks both. Use `createFont({ family, size, lineHeight, weight, letterSpacing, transform, style, color })` inside `createTamagui({ fonts: { body, heading } })`.

payload.principle: Portal: renders children in a different part of the tree
payload.explanation: `stackZIndex` auto-manages layering across nested portals. `passThrough` renders without portal. For native: install `react-native-teleport` and import `@tamagui/native/setup-teleport` at entry to preserve React context inside Sheet/Dialog/Popover. Without it custom contexts aren't available in portaled content. Alternative: use `scope` prop to co-locate trigger and content.

payload.principle: Granular per-property animations via object syntax
payload.explanation: `transition={{ x: 'bouncy', y: { type: 'bouncy', overshootClamping: true } }}` — only x/y animate, each with its own config. Use the array form `transition={['bouncy', { y: 'slow', scale: { type: 'fast', repeat: 2 } }]}` to set a default plus per-prop overrides.

payload.principle: Delay animations via the array syntax
payload.explanation: `transition={['bouncy', { delay: 200 }]}` — delay in milliseconds, works across all drivers. Stagger children with `transition={['bouncy', { delay: i * 100 }]}`. CSS driver uses `transition-delay`; Motion converts to seconds internally.

payload.principle: Themes are objects of contextual values resolved upwards
payload.explanation: Define themes with any keys: `{ background: '#000', color: '#fff' }`. Style props that use `$key` first look up the theme, then fall back through parent themes, ultimately to tokens. Themes are like scoped CSS variables vs tokens which are global. Use `useTheme()` hook to read values in code.

payload.principle: Install core for style-only, tamagui for full UI kit
payload.explanation: `yarn add @tamagui/core` gives the styling library alone (~28Kb). `yarn add tamagui` is a superset that adds the full UI kit (Button, Form, Stack, Paragraph, etc.) — anywhere docs say `@tamagui/core` you can swap to `tamagui`. Use `@tamagui/config` for a recommended preset (`defaultConfig` from `@tamagui/config/v5`).

payload.principle: Type themes with `satisfies` and a BaseTheme anchor
payload.explanation: Put themes in a separate file. Define `const light = { ... }`, derive `type BaseTheme = typeof light`, then declare the rest `const dark: BaseTheme = { ... }`. Export all themes with `satisfies { [key: string]: BaseTheme }` so TypeScript catches missing keys while preserving exact literal types. Requires TS 4.9+.

payload.principle: Group containers on native need untilMeasured or fixed dims
payload.explanation: Native can't know a component's layout before first render. Add `untilMeasured="hide"` on the parent to start invisible until measured, preventing flicker. Alternatively set explicit width/height on the container so children can match a container query on first paint and avoid the double render altogether.

payload.principle: Button.Apply enables group-based interactive theming
payload.explanation: Set `group="btn"` on the Button frame and use `$group-btn-hover`/`$group-btn-press` on Button.Text or Button.Icon children to coordinate hover/press visual state across sub-components. This replaces the old pattern of prop-drilling active states.

payload.principle: .styleable() wraps functional components for use with styled()
payload.explanation: When building a compound component, your functional component needs `.styleable()` wrapper so it can be passed to `styled()` later: `const Button = ButtonFrame.styleable(({children, ...props}, ref) => <ButtonFrame ref={ref} {...props}><ButtonText>{children}</ButtonText></ButtonFrame>)`. This ensures prop forwarding, variant merging, and ref forwarding work correctly.

payload.principle: unstyled prop removes all Tamagui default styles
payload.explanation: Every Tamagui UI component accepts `unstyled` — removes all default padding, colors, border radius etc. Useful when you want the accessibility/behavior layer without the visual defaults. Pair with your own styled() wrapper to add custom styling.

payload.principle: Text truncation: ellipsis prop and numberOfLines
payload.explanation: `ellipsis` = shorthand for `numberOfLines={1}`. On web: `text-overflow: ellipsis` + `white-space: nowrap` + `overflow: hidden`. On native: `numberOfLines={1}`. `numberOfLines={3}` → multi-line clamp via `-webkit-line-clamp` on web, native `numberOfLines` on native. Both work on Text, SizableText, and Paragraph.

payload.principle: Variants are the only sanctioned way to share conditional styles
payload.explanation: Variants live inside `styled()` and receive a value + (tokens, theme, props, font, context). They run outside the render function — no hooks, clear contract, type-safe, compiler-friendly. They nest without adding tree depth. Use `as const` on the variant object until TS can infer constant objects. Alternative (free-form logic in a component) de-opts the compiler.

payload.principle: Tamagui is a universal React styling framework with one dep: React
payload.explanation: Tamagui core is a React-only styling framework (~28Kb on web) that supports the full React Native View/Text API, a superset of RN StyleSheet, styled(), hooks, and typed design-system helpers. Runs at runtime and compile-time without the usual zero-runtime library limits. Unstyled and styled versions of all components.

payload.principle: Tabs: horizontal/vertical, automatic or manual activation
payload.explanation: `activationMode="automatic"` selects on focus; `"manual"` requires click/Enter. Anatomy: `Tabs > Tabs.List > Tabs.Tab + Tabs.Content`. Three modes: Styled (default), Unstyled (`createTabs`), Headless (`useTabs` hook with no core dep). `orientation` for vertical layout. Full keyboard navigation with looping.

payload.principle: Dynamic variants: a single function instead of object syntax
payload.explanation: For maximum flexibility: `variants: { doubleMargin: (val: number) => ({ margin: val * 2 }) }`. Shorter than the object-with-keys form but less compiler-optimizable than typed spread variants. Use when types are complex or the calculation is essential.

payload.principle: createStyledContext shares variant props across compound components
payload.explanation: For compound APIs like `<Button.Icon>`/`<Button.Text>`, `createStyledContext()` scopes shared variants (e.g. `size`) across siblings. Pass the context via `context: ButtonContext` on each `styled()`; wrap with `<Button.Props size="$large">` to set from above. Caveat: using context currently disables compiler flattening — keep it off hot primitives like Stack/Text.

payload.principle: Native integrations are opt-in: gestures, menus, toast, linear-gradient
payload.explanation: All native integrations follow the same pattern: install the package, import its setup module before Tamagui in your app entry, optionally pass a `native` prop to the component. Gesture handler: better press detection + smoother Sheet; native menus: iOS/Android system context menus with haptics; native LinearGradient: expo-linear-gradient for GPU-accelerated gradients.

payload.principle: SSR three-phase: server CSS → hydration match → client animations
payload.explanation: Phase 1: server renders with CSS media queries and variables (no JS). Phase 2: client hydrates to exactly match server output (no flicker). Phase 3: after hydration, spring animations activate. SSR is auto-detected via TamaguiProvider's config; produce CSS with `config.getCSS()` or bundler plugins and serve it in the initial HTML.

payload.principle: Card: themeable container with Header, Footer, Background sub-components
payload.explanation: `size` prop on Card cascades to padding, border radius, and child sizing. `elevate` boolean adds visual elevation. `Card.Background` renders behind content at lower z-index — useful for images or gradients. All sub-components accept `unstyled` to remove Tamagui defaults.

payload.principle: Checkbox: tri-state (true/false/indeterminate), headless available
payload.explanation: Supports `checked` values: `true`, `false`, `'indeterminate'`. Indeterminate is useful for select-all patterns. `native` prop renders HTML checkbox. Headless mode via `@tamagui/checkbox-headless` + `useCheckbox` hook returns `checkboxProps`, `checkboxRef`, hidden form input — zero Tamagui dependency.

payload.principle: Pseudo-state transitions: CSS-like enter/exit semantics
payload.explanation: Set `transition` at the base and override inside `hoverStyle`/`pressStyle`/`focusStyle`. Entering the pseudo uses the pseudo's transition; leaving it uses the base transition. Priority follows CSS specificity: press > hover > focus. Works with group-pseudo variants like `$group-card-hover`.

payload.principle: Tree-flattening converts styled components into native DOM/View nodes
payload.explanation:
A `` becomes a `
` on web and `` on native; `` becomes `

`. This removes styled-component wrapper depth from the React tree, cutting React reconciliation cost. Across real apps 30-50% of components typically flatten — mark up with `// debug` pragma to see exactly what gets flattened.

payload.principle: v2 breaking change: animation → transition, space → gap, tag → render
payload.explanation: Renamed props in v2: `animation` prop → `transition`, `space`/`spaceDirection` → `gap`, `tag` → `render`, `Stack` → `View`, `ellipse` → `numberOfLines`. Shadow props removed — use `boxShadow`. All RN accessibility props → ARIA equivalents. `Group` children now need `Group.Item` wrapper. `Input` migrated to web-standard HTML attrs.

payload.principle: Use Motion on web + Reanimated on native via platform files
payload.explanation: Create `animations.ts` (Motion) and `animations.native.ts` (Reanimated), both exporting the same shape. Bundler picks the right file. Keeps web bundles small while getting off-thread native animations. Works the same for any cross-platform driver pairing (e.g. CSS + RN).

payload.principle: transition prop activates animations — keep it on once used
payload.explanation: The `transition` prop names an animation config key. Spring hooks are expensive so Tamagui only enables them when `transition` is present; if you ever set it, the component keeps the hook for its lifetime. To disable mid-life, pass `null`/`false`/`undefined` — do NOT spread it conditionally. To remove/add after mount, change the component `key`.

payload.principle: Popover: floating pane with 12 placements and arrow
payload.explanation: Anatomy: `Popover > Popover.Trigger + Popover.Content > Popover.Arrow`. 12 placement options (top/bottom/left/right × center/start/end). `allowFlip` repositions when no space. `stayInFrame` adds 10px viewport padding. `transformOrigin: true` auto-calculates for scale animations. On mobile, adapt to Sheet.

payload.principle: V5 ships matching animation keys across all drivers
payload.explanation: The v5 preset defines the same keys (fast, medium, slow, bouncy, lazy, quick, …) across CSS/RN/Reanimated/Motion drivers so code written once runs everywhere. Swap drivers by changing an import — no per-component edits needed.

payload.principle: Cross-platform CSS shorthand with $variables in strings
payload.explanation: `boxShadow`, `backgroundImage`, `filter`, `border`, `outline` accept string shorthand with token refs: `boxShadow="0 0 10px $shadowColor"`, `backgroundImage="linear-gradient(to bottom, $background, $color)"`, `filter="blur($2)"`, `border="1px solid $borderColor"`. Web: emits CSS vars. Native: expands to individual props (borderWidth/Style/Color). url() in backgroundImage is web-only.

payload.principle: Tamagui compiler does 4 things: atomic CSS, inline hoisting, tree-flattening, hook evaluation
payload.explanation: @tamagui/static analyses JSX at build time via partial evaluation: (1) extracts all style props into atomic CSS classes; (2) hoists constants out of the render loop; (3) flattens styled() wrappers into plain div/View nodes; (4) evaluates useMedia/useTheme calls into CSS media queries and variables. Result: smaller JS bundle, faster runtime, native primitives on web.

payload.principle: defaultVariants sets typed defaults for variants
payload.explanation: TS can't infer variant types on the object they're defined on (left-to-right parsing). Put defaults in the separate `defaultVariants` key — properly typed: `defaultVariants: { size: '$10' }`. The component renders with those values when the prop is omitted.

payload.principle: Pseudo style props: hoverStyle, pressStyle, focusStyle, disabledStyle
payload.explanation: Inline pseudo-state objects: `hoverStyle` (web, :hover), `pressStyle` (while pressed), `focusStyle` (:focus), `focusVisibleStyle` (:focus-visible, keyboard), `focusWithinStyle` (:focus-within on descendants), `disabledStyle` (disabled prop true). Each accepts any Tamagui style props. Compiles to atomic CSS on web; runtime on native.

payload.principle: transition applies to all animatable styles by default
payload.explanation: `transition="bouncy"` animates every changed style prop. Like CSS `transition: all`. Combine with `animateOnly={['opacity','scale']}` to restrict the set. On web with the CSS driver, the compiler can fully extract this into class-based transitions.

payload.principle: render prop: change the rendered element type
payload.explanation: v2 replaces `tag` with `render`. Three forms: (1) string tag name `render="a"` — compiler-optimized; (2) JSX element `render={<a href="/">}` — not compiler-optimizable (deopt); (3) render function `render={(props) => <a {...props} />}` — full control. For HTML anchor links use `<View render="a" href="/" />`.

payload.principle: themeInverse flips light↔dark within any subtree
payload.explanation: `<View themeInverse>` inverts the current theme for the subtree. If the root is dark, the children get light; vice versa. Useful for contrast sections or call-to-action areas that need to stand out from the rest of the page.

payload.principle: useTheme proxies the current theme with per-key tracking
payload.explanation: `const theme = useTheme()` returns the current theme resolved up through parents. Values are Variable objects with `.val`, `.variable`, `.name`. On web, `theme.bg.get()` returns the CSS variable reference (no re-render on theme change); on native it returns the value and re-renders. Pass component/theme names as args to target a specific sub-theme.

payload.principle: animateOnly restricts which style keys animate
payload.explanation: Pass an array of prop names: `animateOnly={['transform', 'opacity']}` so the animation driver only handles those. Useful when a component has many changing props but only a few should tween.

payload.principle: useProps and useStyle hooks extract computed values from styled components
payload.explanation: `useProps(props)` returns the fully-resolved props after merging variants and theme. `useStyle(props)` returns just the style object. `usePropsAndStyle(props)` returns both. Useful when you need to pass resolved styles to third-party components or for debugging what a component actually produces.

payload.principle: Universal + Compiler solves the frontend trilemma
payload.explanation: Cross-platform React historically forces choose-two-of-three: native feel, native+web deploy, code sharing. Tamagui's optimizing compiler extracts styles, evaluates useTheme/useMedia into CSS variables + media queries, and flattens trees — so you can write once, share ~60-90% code, and still feel native. Gains come from atomic CSS + tree-flattening.

payload.principle: FontLanguage: context-based font switching via CSS variables
payload.explanation: `<FontLanguage body="cn"><Text fontFamily="$body">你好</Text></FontLanguage>`. Define variant fonts with suffix: `body_cn: createFont({…})`. On web uses CSS vars — no re-renders when switching. Reset to default with `<FontLanguage body="default">`. Works with `styled()` — define `fontFamily="$body"` once and language switches transparently.

payload.principle: Variants can include pseudo and media styles
payload.explanation: Inside a variant value you can nest media-query and pseudo blocks: `size: { md: { fontSize: '$sm', $gtMd: { fontSize: '$md' }, hoverStyle: {…} } }`. Variants have the full power of Tamagui's style system.

payload.principle: // debug pragma shows extraction details per file
payload.explanation: Add `// debug` as the first comment in a file to print full compiler output: which components flattened, what CSS was generated, how styles were merged. Also add `// tamagui-ignore` to completely skip extraction for a file, or use the `disableOptimization` prop on a single component.

payload.principle: Sheet: bottom panel with snap points and drag support
payload.explanation: Anatomy: `Sheet > Sheet.Overlay + Sheet.Handle + Sheet.Frame`. `snapPoints` defaults to [80, 10] (percent). `modal={true}` renders at app root. `disableDrag` removes gesture. `moveOnKeyboardChange` shifts sheet up on native keyboard. `dismissOnOverlayPress` defaults true. Install `react-native-gesture-handler` for smoother scroll-to-drag transitions.

payload.principle: AnimatePresence animates children as they unmount
payload.explanation: Wrap conditional children with `<AnimatePresence>` so `exitStyle` runs on unmount. Each child must have a unique `key`. Extra props: `initial` (disable first-mount animation), `custom` (pass data to variants — e.g. directional carousels), `exitBeforeEnter`, `onExitComplete`. Driver-agnostic — works with CSS, RN, Reanimated, Motion.

payload.principle: Motion driver: web-only, GPU via WAAPI
payload.explanation: `@tamagui/animations-motion` + `motion` — uses Web Animations API running on the compositor thread. Supports spring `{ type: 'spring', damping, mass, stiffness }` and tween `{ duration }`. Zero re-renders: Tamagui hands style changes directly to Motion. Pair with Reanimated on native via `.native.ts` file split.

payload.principle: Reanimated driver: off-thread native animations
payload.explanation: `@tamagui/animations-reanimated` + `react-native-reanimated`. Animations run on the UI thread via worklets for smooth 60fps. Tamagui can bypass its internal style changes and hand off directly to Reanimated worklets, avoiding re-renders. Best for complex native apps and gesture-driven UI. Larger bundle, needs RN platform setup.

payload.principle: Vite: @tamagui/vite-plugin requires ESM
payload.explanation: Set `"type": "module"` in package.json. Add to `vite.config.ts`: `tamaguiPlugin({ config: './tamagui.config.ts', components: ['tamagui'] })`. Plugin reads `tamagui.build.ts` for shared config. React Native packages may need additional alias mappings for RNW compatibility.

payload.principle: Expo: use template, babel plugin, and Inter font
payload.explanation: `yarn create tamagui@latest --template expo-router` (Yarn 4.4.0+). Manual: install `@tamagui/babel-plugin`, add to `babel.config.js`. Web: follow Metro guide. Fonts via `@tamagui/font-inter` or `@expo-google-fonts/inter` + `useFonts` hook. Clear cache on first run: `npx expo start -c`. Add `userInterfaceStyle: "automatic"` to app.config.ts for dark mode.

payload.principle: group prop transforms any Stack into a group context
payload.explanation: Adding `group` (or `group="name"`) to a Stack makes it a container query root. Child components can then use `$group-hover`, `$group-press`, `$group-sm` selectors. No import changes needed — it's a built-in prop. Named groups enable `$group-name-hover` cross-component targeting.

payload.principle: enterStyle and exitStyle drive mount/unmount animations
payload.explanation: `enterStyle` = initial styles on mount (animate from). `exitStyle` = target styles on unmount (animate to). Tamagui normalizes opacity/scale to 1 and y to 0 on the base when only defined in enter/exitStyle. Use with `<AnimatePresence>` for unmount; direct children need unique `key` props.

payload.principle: Token categories map to specific style props automatically
payload.explanation: `size` → width/height/minWidth/minHeight/maxWidth/maxHeight. `zIndex` → zIndex. `radius` → borderRadius and each corner. `color` → color/backgroundColor/borderColor/per-side borderColor. `space` → everything else (padding, margin, gap, top, left, etc.). So `<View padding="$4">` looks up `space.$4`.

payload.principle: CSS driver: smallest bundle, transition-based
payload.explanation: `@tamagui/animations-css` — `createAnimations({ fast: 'ease-in 150ms', slow: 'cubic-bezier(0.68,-0.55,0.265,1.55) 300ms' })`. At runtime it sets CSS `transition`; at compile time the compiler extracts it. No spring physics. Best for simple web apps wanting maximum perf and smallest JS.

payload.principle: Separate enter/exit transitions via { enter, exit, default }
payload.explanation: `transition={{ enter: 'lazy', exit: 'quick', default: 'bouncy' }}` — slow entry, fast exit, medium for regular prop changes. Combines with array form: `transition={['bouncy', { enter: 'lazy', exit: 'quick', delay: 200, x: 'slow' }]}`. Works with all four drivers.

payload.principle: Accordion: collapsible sections, single or multiple open
payload.explanation: Anatomy: `Accordion > Accordion.Item > Accordion.Header > Accordion.Trigger + Accordion.Content`. `type="single"` allows only one open at a time (`collapsible` prop lets you close it); `type="multiple"` allows any number. Set `defaultValue` for initial open items. Full keyboard navigation.

payload.principle: View and Text are the Tamagui base primitives
payload.explanation: Import from `tamagui` or `@tamagui/core`. View = RN View + Tamagui style superset + press/hover/focus events (like Pressable). Text = RN Text + Tamagui styles. Both accept inline styles, $-prefix selectors, hoverStyle/pressStyle etc. Use them for inline styling; extend via `styled()` for reusable components. Order of style props is significant.

payload.principle: ClientOnly and useDidFinishSSR for selective client rendering
payload.explanation: `<ClientOnly>` renders children only after hydration — useful for interactive charts or editors where SSR output would mismatch. `useDidFinishSSR()` returns false on server, true after hydration. `useClientValue(val)` returns undefined during SSR then the real value client-side. Disable SSR entirely via config for SPAs.

payload.principle: Inline styles are first-class — the compiler flattens them
payload.explanation: Tamagui encourages inline style props: `<View mx="$sm" scale={1.2}><Text c="$color" /></View>`. With shorthands it is concise and the optimizing compiler hoists/extracts these into CSS at build time — no runtime cost. You don't have to choose between inline and `styled()`.

payload.principle: TamaguiProvider wraps your app with config + defaultTheme
payload.explanation: Wrap the root in `<TamaguiProvider config={config} defaultTheme="light">`. Set the root theme via the `defaultTheme` prop rather than an outer `<Theme>` — this enables `fastSchemeChange` on native and lets light/dark use faster media-query updates during SSR on web.

payload.principle: Vite plugin: @tamagui/vite-plugin, requires ESM
payload.explanation: Install `@tamagui/vite-plugin`, set `"type": "module"` in package.json. Add `tamaguiPlugin({ config: './tamagui.config.ts', components: ['tamagui'] })` to `vite.config.ts`. Reads from `tamagui.build.ts` automatically for shared settings.

payload.principle: Select: accessible dropdown with typeahead, adapts to Sheet on mobile
payload.explanation: Anatomy: `Select > Select.Trigger + Select.Content > Select.Viewport > Select.Item + Select.ItemText`. Keyboard navigable with typeahead. Use `lazyMount` + `renderValue` together for pages with many Selects — defers mounting all items until first open. Must adapt to Sheet on native (no native dropdown API).

payload.principle: Spinner: thin ActivityIndicator wrapper, size limited to small/large
payload.explanation: `<Spinner size="large" color="$green10" />`. Extends YStack so accepts all layout props. Size options limited by RN ActivityIndicator API (small/large only). Works on web and native. Include in buttons during loading state, or standalone as a page loader.

payload.principle: Radix Colors v3 in v5: 10+ palettes, improved accessibility
payload.explanation: V5 config adds orange, pink, purple, teal, gray, neutral color themes built on Radix Colors v3. Includes shadow scales, highlight variants, opacity adjustments. Use `createV5Theme({ palettes: { accent: myPalette } })` to swap any palette. `adjustPalette` utilities transform lightness/saturation programmatically.

payload.principle: React Native driver: spring/timing via Animated, no extra deps
payload.explanation: `@tamagui/animations-react-native` — supports spring physics via `{ damping, mass, stiffness }` and timing via `duration`. Works on web and native, no extra native libraries, slightly bigger than CSS. Runs on-thread — less performant than Reanimated for complex gesture animations.

payload.principle: v2 breaking change: media query naming and flex defaults
payload.explanation: Config v5 media renames: `$2xl` → `$xxl`, `$2xs` → `$xxs`. Max queries: kebab-case (`max-xxl`). New height queries (`height-sm/md/lg`) and device queries (`touch`, `hoverable`). Flex behavior: `flexBasis` now defaults to `0` (RN-aligned). `position` defaults to browser `static` (not `relative`). Bundle size: ~32% smaller (37KB → 25KB gzipped).

payload.principle: Spread variants gather values from a token category
payload.explanation: Instead of enumerating every size, write `pad: { '...size': (val, { tokens }) => ({ padding: tokens.size[val] }) }`. The `...` prefix is required — it types the prop against that token category and removes per-key boilerplate. Allowed: ...color, ...size, ...space, ...font, ...fontSize, ...lineHeight, ...radius, ...letterSpace, ...zIndex.

payload.principle: Partial evaluation handles most const/import patterns
payload.explanation: The compiler evaluates simple expressions at build time: string/number literals, object spreads, ternaries on known booleans, imports from whitelisted files. Add constants files to the `importsWhitelist` in `tamagui.build.ts` to include their values. Complex logic (closures over non-const values, third-party hooks) causes a graceful deopt to runtime.

payload.principle: Slider: range input with multiple thumbs and keyboard control
payload.explanation: Anatomy: `Slider > Slider.Track > Slider.TrackActive + Slider.Thumb`. `defaultValue` is an array — multiple entries = multiple thumbs (range select). `step` controls keyboard increment. On iOS: pass safe area insets to TamaguiProvider or thumb tracking will misalign due to safe area offsets.

payload.principle: Tamagui config declares tokens, themes, media, animations, shorthands
payload.explanation: `createTamagui({ tokens, themes, media, animations, shorthands, fonts, settings })` returns the typed config passed to TamaguiProvider. Use `declare module '@tamagui/core' { interface TamaguiCustomConfig extends typeof config {} }` so props get strong types. Start from `@tamagui/config/v5` preset for sensible defaults, Tailwind-aligned shorthands, full themes.

payload.principle: rem units work cross-platform with PixelRatio.getFontScale()
payload.explanation: Write `<View padding="1rem" />` — on web browsers handle rem; on native Tamagui multiplies by `PixelRatio.getFontScale()` so the user's accessibility font setting scales everything. Configure base with `createTamagui({ settings: { remBaseFontSize: 16 } })`.

payload.principle: Animation drivers are swappable per-platform via config
payload.explanation: Add animations via a driver passed to `createTamagui({ animations: createAnimations({…}) })`. Four drivers: CSS (smallest, easing only), React Native (Animated, spring), Reanimated (off-thread, advanced), Motion (WAAPI, web). Swap drivers using `.native.ts` file splits, a `<Configuration>` wrapper, or multi-driver configs with the `animatedBy` prop.

payload.principle: styled(Component, config) creates an optimizable component
payload.explanation: Extends any Tamagui component with custom styles: `styled(View, { borderRadius: 20, variants: {...} })`. Gives you typed props, compiler-friendly style shape, and variant merging. `styled(styled())` still yields a single React component in the tree — variants and nested styling compose without adding render depth. Don't wrap Pressable/TouchableOpacity — their event logic conflicts.

payload.principle: Tamagui styles are a superset of React Native styles
payload.explanation: Base style props start from ReactNative ViewStyle/TextStyle and add: cross-platform shadow/filter/backgroundImage/border/outline, web-only CSS features, flat transform props (x, y, scale, rotate…), pseudo states, enter/exit, and parent-based selectors. Works via inline props on View/Text and inside `styled()`. Exported types: ViewStyle, TextStyle.

payload.principle: tamagui UI kit standardizes theme keys: background, color, borderColor, shadowColor, placeholderColor
payload.explanation: Tamagui UI components read a fixed set of keys: background, color, borderColor, shadowColor, placeholderColor. Each has pseudo variants: backgroundHover, backgroundPress, backgroundFocus; same for color and borderColor. Define these in every theme to re-theme the whole UI kit together. All are optional — set `unstyled` on any component to skip them.

payload.principle: Tamagui 2 minimum versions: React 19, RN 0.81, TS 5
payload.explanation: Tamagui 2 requires React 19+, React Native 0.81+ with the New Architecture enabled (for native apps), and TypeScript 5+. On web there are no version restrictions. These requirements unlock new RN style features like cross-platform boxShadow, filter, backgroundImage, and border shorthand.

payload.principle: useMedia() is a signal-like hook — only subscribes to accessed keys
payload.explanation: `const media = useMedia()` returns a proxy that tracks which keys you access; re-renders only when those keys change. The compiler can fully extract usages and remove the hook from output. Proxy constraints: not iterable, no `in`/Object.keys, no destructuring with renaming. Access keys directly like `media.sm` for best compiler support.

payload.principle: Next.js + CLI is the modern path for Turbopack
payload.explanation: For Next.js with Turbopack: `yarn add -D @tamagui/cli`. Build script: `tamagui build --target web ./src -- next build`. CLI pre-compiles files in place and restores them after. For legacy Webpack Next.js use `@tamagui/next-plugin` instead.

payload.principle: Image: SSR-safe cross-platform with pluggable backend
payload.explanation: `<Image src="..." width={200} height={200} objectFit="cover" />`. Supports `loading="lazy"`, `fetchPriority`, `srcSet`/`sizes` on web. `alt` required for accessibility. Use `createImage` to integrate expo-image for blurhash placeholders, transitions, and CDN caching while keeping Tamagui's style API.

payload.principle: Metro/React Native: @tamagui/babel-plugin is fully optional
payload.explanation: Tamagui works without any babel config on native — just `TamaguiProvider`. The babel plugin adds compiler optimizations: CSS extraction isn't applicable, but it can flatten styled components and hoist constants. Configure in `babel.config.js` with `config`, `components`, and `exclude: /node_modules/`.

payload.principle: Input/TextArea: web-first API with native mapping
payload.explanation: v2 Inputs use HTML input attributes directly — `type="password"`, `type="email"`, `enterKeyHint` for keyboard return key. `onChange` is preferred over `onChangeText`. On native Tamagui maps these to RN equivalents. TextArea uses `rows` instead of `numberOfLines`. Migration from v1: `secureTextEntry` → `type="password"`, `keyboardType="email-address"` → `type="email"`.
